rationale: The citing paper uses Grinstein and Kronrod (2016) in the Study 4 setup to support a specific theoretical claim: forceful or assertive language can trigger reactance. That citation is tied to the authors' own warning-versus-notification framing manipulation and their prediction about conservative backlash to government messages. Even though it appears in a grouped citation, it plays a clear role in motivating hypothesis logic and design rather than serving as a generic mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's communication-tone insight (more forceful wording can backfire via reactance) and applies it to government warning-label framing. It uses that idea to justify testing warning versus notification language in a new political-ideology context.
